violations.text: Hotdogs were observed thawing in stagnant water. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Hotdogs were placed in oven at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/03/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. PIC stated that application has been submitted. New Violation. Correct By: 09/24/2024
The condensate from the milk cooler is draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 10/09/2024

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 400 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be 200 ppm. PIC diluted at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC took out of service to be cleaned and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2024
